title: vSAN Cluster Capacity Headroom and Slack Space
exclusions: The templated stub sourcetype `vsan:diskgroup` (and older boilerplate) conflated per–disk group utilization with cluster-wide admission and slack math. Production: `vmware:vsan:capacity` is the custom HEC name for the REST (or SOAP) cluster capacity object; use `vmware:vsan:health` for the capacity-test and object-quorum slice; re-use `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` (UC-19.2.17) only for optional per-DG distribution drill when that feed exists. The stub did not model largest host rebuild headroom, SPBM policy_reserve_capacity, or 7.0 U1 / 7.0 U3 / 8.0 reserve model differences—this use case does. This UC is not per-DG mount / checksum (UC-19.2.17), not witness (UC-19.2.23), not component resync depth (sibling UC-19.2.30 when present), and not Nutanix (UC-19.2.25) or Azure HCI (UC-19.2.21). CIM models are not applied—omit CIM datamodels here.
evidence: Saved search `uc_19_2_27_vsan_cluster_capacity` on `index=vmware` or `index=hci` with HEC to those indexes, 13+ months online retention, linked vsan_cluster_capacity_owner.csv in Git, plus alert run history in `audit_evidence` or the ticketing system. Attach the vCenter vSAN Capacity screenshot for a sampled alert row in post-incident reviews.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab, shrink free space to force **usable_used_pct** above the lookup **capacity_critical_pct** and confirm a **critical** `severity_bucket` on the next 300s poll; separately inject a **vmware:vsan:health** line with a red capacity test for the same `(vcenter, cluster)` and ensure **cap_health_tier** alone can drive **critical** even if bytes look better on stale capacity rows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Set **change_freeze_until** in **vsan_cluster_capacity_owner.csv** to a future time; reload; confirm **high** and **medium** results become **ok** in `severity_bucket` while a simulated **red** vSAN health flag still yields **critical**; clear the freeze, restore prior behaviour.

dataSources: PRIMARY: `(index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity` — newline JSON, one cluster per poll (300s), from a Python 3 + pyVmomi or vSphere REST poller. REST: `GET https://<vcenter>/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity` (vSphere 7.0 U2+) emitting `total_bytes`, `free_bytes`, `used_bytes`, `reserved_capacity` (operations + host-rebuild slices where exposed), `efficiency_breakdown` (dedup and compression), `policy_reserve_capacity` / SPBM slice; `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}` for `dedup_enabled`, `compression_enabled`, `data_efficiency_state`, `version`, `host_count` where present; `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/host` to compute **largest host** footprint for `largestHostCapacityB` / `largest_host_capacity_bytes`. SOAP (older / REST gap): `vsanQuerySpaceUsage` (VsanSpaceUsage) for `totalCapacityB`, `freeCapacityB`, `efficientCapacity`, `efficiencyBreakdown` via the vSAN-Health service stub, registered with the **vsan-health** port — same HEC line shape after the poller flattens to snake_case / camelCase mirrors. SECONDARY: Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 3215, https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215) for `vmware:vc:event` with `eventTypeId` in families such as `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.clusteryellowred.*` and `esx.problem.vob.vsan.lsom.*` (capacity-adjacent health events). Also Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380, https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380) for the same event families. TERTIARY: `vmware:vsan:health` from a second poller to `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/health` filtered to `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.clustercapacity.utilization` and `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.objectquorum.objectquorum` (slack-aware health-service view). OPTIONAL drill-only: `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` (UC-19.2.17 contract) is **not** used for the primary capacity severity; keep it for per–disk group distribution when you already run that feed.
app: No stock Splunk TA publishes vmware:vsan:capacity. Combine: (1) custom HEC from this repo’s Python 3.10+ poller on a Heavy Forwarder (pyVmomi, requests to vCenter REST, optional vSAN-Health SOAP). (2) Splunk Add-on for VMware ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) for `vmware:vc:event` gap-fill. (3) Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder ([Splunkbase 5380](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)) for modern forwarder `vmware:vc:event` when you cannot run the HEC poller. See HEC: https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/UsetheHEC — pyVmomi: https://github.com/vmware/pyvmomi — REST reference in VMware vSphere / vSAN documentation.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.27 — vSAN cluster-level capacity headroom, slack/operations+host-rebuild reserve, usable_used_pct, host-rebuild headroom, ETA-to-full at 5m cadence. PRIMARY sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity from GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity (v7.0 U2+) or SOAP vsanQuerySpaceUsage; the base search union lists vmware:vsan:health (capacity-test slice) plus vmware:vsan:diskgroup (UC-19.2.17 drill contract); the numeric pipeline filters to vmware:vsan:capacity. NOT UC-19.2.17 per-DG state, NOT UC-19.2.23 witness, NOT per-DG used_pct for admission—usable_used_pct vs slack is the control plane.")`
(index=vmware OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup") earliest=-25h@h
| eval cluster=toString(coalesce(cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
| eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, vcenter_fqdn, ""))
| eval total_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_bytes, totalCapacityB, total_capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval free_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(free_bytes, freeCapacityB, free_capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval used_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_bytes, usedCapacityB, used_capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval reserved_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(reserved_bytes, reservedCapacityB, reserved_capacity_bytes, policy_reserve_capacity, policyReserveCapacity, "0")), 10)
| eval largest_host_capacity_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(largest_host_capacity_bytes, largestHostCapacityB, largestHostCapacity, "0")), 10)
| eval host_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_count, hostCount, "0")), 10)
| eval dedup_enabled=if((lower(toString(coalesce(dedup_enabled, dedupEnabled, "true")))="true") OR (toString(coalesce(dedup_enabled, dedupEnabled, "0"))="1"), 1, 0)
| eval dedup_ratio=tonumber(toString(coalesce(dedup_ratio, dedupRatio, efficiency_dedup_ratio, dedup_ratio_observed, "0")), 10)
| where (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity") AND (isnotnull(total_bytes)) AND (total_bytes>0)
| sort 0 + _time, vcenter, cluster
| streamstats window=288 current=t first(used_bytes) AS used_24h_ago avg(used_bytes) AS avg_used_bytes range(used_bytes) AS range_used_bytes BY vcenter, cluster
| eval used_growth_bytes_per_day=if((isnotnull(used_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(used_bytes)) AND (used_bytes>=used_24h_ago), used_bytes - used_24h_ago, 0)
| eval used_pct=round((used_bytes / total_bytes) * 100, 1)
| eval free_pct=round((free_bytes / total_bytes) * 100, 1)
| eval reserve_pct=if((isnotnull(reserved_bytes)) AND (total_bytes>0) AND (reserved_bytes>=0), round((reserved_bytes / total_bytes) * 100, 1), null())
| eval usable_capacity_bytes=if(isnotnull(reserved_bytes), total_bytes - reserved_bytes, total_bytes)
| eval usable_used_pct=if((isnotnull(usable_capacity_bytes)) AND (usable_capacity_bytes>0) AND (isnotnull(used_bytes)), round((used_bytes / usable_capacity_bytes) * 100, 1), null())
| eval headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes=if((isnotnull(free_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(largest_host_capacity_bytes)), free_bytes - largest_host_capacity_bytes, null())
| eval eta_full_days=if((used_growth_bytes_per_day>0) AND (isnotnull(free_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(reserved_bytes)) AND ((free_bytes - reserved_bytes) > 0), round((free_bytes - reserved_bytes) / used_growth_bytes_per_day, 1), null())
| stats max(_time) AS _time latest(used_bytes) AS used_bytes latest(free_bytes) AS free_bytes latest(used_24h_ago) AS used_24h_ago latest(used_growth_bytes_per_day) AS used_growth_bytes_per_day latest(used_pct) AS used_pct latest(free_pct) AS free_pct latest(reserve_pct) AS reserve_pct latest(usable_capacity_bytes) AS usable_capacity_bytes latest(usable_used_pct) AS usable_used_pct latest(headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes) AS headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes latest(eta_full_days) AS eta_full_days latest(dedup_ratio) AS dedup_ratio latest(dedup_enabled) AS dedup_enabled latest(largest_host_capacity_bytes) AS largest_host_capacity_bytes latest(reserved_bytes) AS reserved_bytes latest(avg_used_bytes) AS avg_used_bytes latest(range_used_bytes) AS range_used_bytes latest(host_count) AS host_count latest(total_bytes) AS total_bytes BY vcenter, cluster
| join type=left vcenter, cluster [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" earliest=-25h@h | eval h_test=lower(toString(coalesce(health_test_id, healthTestId, test_id, testId, ""))) | eval h_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(health_test_status, test_status, testStatus, overallStatus, resultStatus, "")))) | eval cluster=toString(coalesce(cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, cluster_id, clusterId, "")) | eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, "")) | where (like(h_test, "%clustercapacity%utilization%")) OR (like(h_test, "%objectquorum%objectquorum%")) OR (h_test="com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.clustercapacity.utilization") OR (h_test="com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.objectquorum.objectquorum") | eval w=if((like(h_status, "%red%")) OR (h_status="failed") OR (h_status="error"), 3, if((like(h_status, "%yellow%")) OR (h_status="warning"), 2, 1)) | stats max(w) AS w BY vcenter, cluster | eval cap_health_tier=case((w>2), "red", w=2, "yellow", 1=1, "green") | fields vcenter, cluster, cap_health_tier ]
| lookup vsan_cluster_capacity_owner cluster OUTPUT business_service, owner_email, expected_dedup_ratio, expected_host_count, change_freeze_until, capacity_target_pct, capacity_warn_pct, capacity_critical_pct, target_resync_window_hours
| eval t_crit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(capacity_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
| eval t_warn=tonumber(toString(coalesce(capacity_warn_pct, "80")), 10)
| eval t_tgt=tonumber(toString(coalesce(capacity_target_pct, "70")), 10)
| eval exp_dedup=tonumber(toString(expected_dedup_ratio), 10)
| eval exp_hc=tonumber(toString(expected_host_count), 10)
| eval cap_health_tier=if((isnotnull(cap_health_tier)) AND (len(toString(cap_health_tier))>0), cap_health_tier, "green")
| eval dedup_drift=if((isnotnull(dedup_ratio)) AND (dedup_ratio>0) AND (isnotnull(exp_dedup)) AND (exp_dedup>0) AND (dedup_ratio < (exp_dedup * 0.75)), 1, 0)
| eval low_host=if((isnotnull(exp_hc)) AND (exp_hc>0) AND (isnotnull(host_count)) AND (host_count < exp_hc), 1, 0)
| eval reserve_bloat=if((isnotnull(reserve_pct)) AND (reserve_pct > 30), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((cap_health_tier="red") OR (isnotnull(usable_used_pct) AND (usable_used_pct >= t_crit)) OR (isnotnull(headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes) AND (headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes < 0)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_full_days)) AND (eta_full_days < 7)), "critical", (cap_health_tier="yellow") OR (isnotnull(usable_used_pct) AND (usable_used_pct >= t_warn)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_full_days)) AND (eta_full_days < 30)) OR ((isnotnull(headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(largest_host_capacity_bytes)) AND (largest_host_capacity_bytes>0) AND (headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes < (largest_host_capacity_bytes * 0.25))), "high", (isnotnull(usable_used_pct) AND (usable_used_pct >= t_tgt)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_full_days)) AND (eta_full_days < 60)) OR (dedup_drift=1), "medium", (low_host=1) OR (reserve_bloat=1), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now() < coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (severity_bucket!="critical"), "ok", severity_bucket)
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical", 1, severity_bucket="high", 2, severity_bucket="medium", 3, severity_bucket="low", 4, 1=1, 5)
| convert ctime(_time) AS last_poll
| sort 0 sev_order, -usable_used_pct, cluster
| table _time, last_poll, vcenter, cluster, severity_bucket, sev_order, cap_health_tier, usable_used_pct, used_pct, free_pct, reserve_pct, headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes, largest_host_capacity_bytes, eta_full_days, used_growth_bytes_per_day, dedup_drift, low_host, reserve_bloat, business_service, owner_email, freeze_active, t_crit, t_warn, t_tgt
description: Ingests cluster-aggregate vSAN space and reserve context as `vmware:vsan:capacity` (custom HEC), with optional `vmware:vsan:health` capacity-test status joined by `(vcenter, cluster)`, not per–disk group mounted state (UC-19.2.17). The SPL unifies camelCase and snake_case with `coalesce()` on `cluster` / `vcenter` keys and byte fields, then filters the numeric pipeline to `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity`, applies `streamstats` window=288 (5 min × 288 = 24h) to derive `used_growth_bytes_per_day`, computes usable_used_pct = used / (total − reserved) to reflect operations reserve, host-rebuild reserve, and SPBM policy reserve instead of a naive `used / total` headline, and computes headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes = free_bytes − largest_host_capacity_bytes so you see whether a single large-host loss can be rebuilt. Five-tier severity_bucket uses lookup `vsan_cluster_capacity_owner.csv`, freeze demotion, and a join summarizing the worst of vSAN health capacity and object-quorum tests. This scope differs from UC-19.2.17 (per-DG state/health), UC-19.2.23 (stretched witness / quorum on a different resource), UC-19.2.13 (VxRail Manager LCM, not the raw vSAN free-space object), and UC-19.2.21 (Azure Stack HCI / S2D). This is the third verified vSAN UC in the package after .17 and .23, completing state, witness, and capacity axes.
value: When slack and rebuild reserves are ignored, a cluster can still “look” two-thirds full on a dashboard while admission control and vSAN health already block storage vMotion, disk grow, and snapshot creation—operations that applications and backup tools assume are always on. Sustained pressure on usable_used_pct also narrows the window to absorb resyncs after a host or disk event, so fault-tolerance and maintenance objectives slip before raw TiB look exhausted. A cluster-wide signal that pairs per-cluster threshold overrides (cold versus hot tiers), time-to-full, host-failure headroom, and dedup drift (VDI, mixed-tenant) gives the business lead time to fund hosts or disk groups, coordinate evacuations, and avoid surprise freeze-outs during month-end or quarter close.
implementation: On a Heavy Forwarder: (1) install pyVmomi in a venv, deploy `vsan_capacity_poll.py` every 300s to HEC with `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity` (plus optional `vmware:vsan:health` poller for `/health`); (2) add `lookups/vsan_cluster_capacity_owner.csv`, `default/transforms.conf` stanza; (3) align cluster keys and `host_count` with `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/host`; (4) schedule the saved search every 5–10m on `earliest=-25h@h`; (5) throttle non-critical re-notifications. Pair with UC-19.2.17 for disk-group state if you also run `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` for drill.
visualization: Row 1: composite KPIs — usable_used_pct, eta_full_days, headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes / largest_host_capacity_bytes for one vSAN cluster. Row 2: column chart of used_bytes, free_bytes, reserved_bytes stacked over 30d, plus a band for target_resync_window_hours-style commentary as an annotation, not a chart axis. Row 3: table of this use case’s `table` output with colour rules. Row 4: optional per–disk group from `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` in a child panel when UC-19.2.17 is on.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380)
Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: A capacity-add in flight can keep raw `used_pct` high for 24–48h (sometimes longer in stretched clusters) until rebalancing redistributes data—headline usage looks worse than the steady-state SLO. SPBM `objectSpaceReservation=100` makes the store accounting for space jump with little guest use-time change. Backup tools that run long snapshot chains (common agentless patterns) temporarily inflate the consumed footprint. Deduplication can fall when a tenant lands non-dedupable data (for example always-on guest encryption) even though the cluster is operationally sound. vSphere 8.0 ESA clusters expose a different `efficiency_breakdown` than OSA; align expected_dedup_ratio per cluster to architecture. A vSAN Stretched cluster can show total_bytes and mirror accounting that look awkward next to a single site—`free_bytes` in the poller is still the correct numerator for a linear eta_full_days when your feed is consistent.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

vSAN slack space and rebuild headroom are not a single slider in a legacy Excel sheet — they are the sum of the pieces VMware subtracts from raw `total_bytes` before admission control and vSAN health will allow storage vMotion, VMDK grow, snapshots, and new VMs. Raw `used / total` can read 60% while usable_used_pct = used / (total − reserved_bytes) is 90%, and that is the number that makes DRS-style placement reject work. vSphere 6.7 era guidance often quoted ~30% free for resync comfort; on 7.0 U1 the platform moved to SPBM-driven, policy-mixed auto reservation, which changes the reserved slice with workload policy rather than a fixed 30% guess. On 7.0 U3 and 8.0 you see operations reserve and host-rebuild reserve as separate accounting items (naming in API and UI can change between OSA and ESA). 8.0 refines the story again—never paste thresholds from a blog about 6.7 into a 8.0 ESA cluster without re-baselining. Deduplication and compression change logical-to-physical ratios; if expected_dedup_ratio in the lookup is far above the observed dedup_ratio, capacity forecasts and finance models are off even when no hardware failed.

Differentiation to keep scope honest: UC-19.2.17 is per–disk group state and congestion; this use case is cluster-aggregate capacity headroom and slack — both can be green/red independently. UC-19.2.23 is stretched witness and quorum on a separate host object; you can be fine on witness while the data cluster is out of free space for rebuilds, and the inverse. UC-19.2.13 (VxRail Manager) is control-plane and LCM; Dell customers should run both that and the underlying vSAN capacity feed here. UC-19.2.21 (Azure Stack HCI) is a different vendor and S2D model—re-use the *idea* of reserve math, not the sourcetypes.

vSphere 7.0 U2+ is the floor for the convenience REST `/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity`; earlier builds use pyVmomi + vSAN-Health SOAP in the poller, still tagged `vmware:vsan:capacity`. The HCL still matters: vSAN ReadyNodes are the supported building blocks; a white-box host can make capacity math *look* fine on paper while the vendor will not support the failure mode. Pillar mapping: Capacity is primary, Availability is secondary here because admission and rebuild failure modes become unavailable writes and migrations for dependent apps.

Three Splunk data paths, pick the (1) primary when you can: (1) custom HEC poller, `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity`, 300s; (2) `vmware:vc:event` from [Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215) and/or the vSphere forwarder [Splunkbase 5380](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380) with the vSAN yellow/red and lsom families—coarse, but a safety net; (3) a parallel `vmware:vsan:health` poller to `/health`, filtered to the clustercapacity and objectquorum test ids for the vSAN service’s own red/yellow view.

Service account: a single read-only vCenter user with vSAN read and Storage views, not shared with interactive admins. Polling is 5 minutes so vCenter session use stays predictable (order-of-magnitude ~80 concurrent sessions per user on many builds—treat the service account as a budget). HEC is on an indexer or intermediate Heavy Forwarder with TLS and a rotating token in a vault.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

`inputs.conf` (Heavy Forwarder; not the vCenter appliance):

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_vmware_vsan_cap/local/vsan_capacity_poll.py]
python.version = python3
interval = 300
sourcetype = vmware:vsan:capacity
index = vmware
source = vsan:capacity:poll
disabled = 0
```

A second file or stanza for `vmware:vsan:health` can call `/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/health` with the test-id filter in code if you do not want a join-only backfill. HEC alternative: a tiny flask sidecar is acceptable if your security team forbids the Splunk ExecProcessor; still ship the same JSON.

`props.conf` (Indexer or first parsing tier):

```ini
[vmware:vsan:capacity]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TIME_PREFIX = "poll_utc":\s*"
```

If the poller only sends HEC at ingest time, omit TIME_ in dev, then add `"poll_utc":` after you prove the clock skew to NTP is <30s on vCenter and the collector host. UTF-8 in file inputs; HEC is already JSON.

`lookups/vsan_cluster_capacity_owner.csv`: columns (header row) `cluster, vcenter, business_service, owner_email, expected_dedup_ratio, expected_host_count, change_freeze_until, capacity_target_pct, capacity_warn_pct, capacity_critical_pct, target_resync_window_hours`. Use 0-filled optional cells only where the SPL treats 0 as “no override”; leave capacity\_\* columns blank to inherit 70 / 80 / 90 in the search. The `target_resync_window_hours` is your written RTO for a host-loss event—surface it in dashboards, not in the base case statement, unless you extend the SPL. Version-control the CSV in the same app as the saved search with a CAB hook for threshold edits. `transforms.conf`: a stanza `[vsan_cluster_capacity_owner]` with `filename = vsan_cluster_capacity_owner.csv`, `min_matches = 0`, case-insensitive for `cluster`.

Python (outline, ~90 lines, no inline comments in production code per your build rule—keep logging via `logging` module to files instead of # lines):

```python
import json, os, ssl, time, sys, logging, urllib.request
from pyVim import connect
from pyVmomi import vim
CA = os.environ.get("VCENTER_CA", "/etc/pki/company/vc-chain.pem")
HEC = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_URL", "https://idx:8088/services/collector/event")
HECTOK = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN", "")
def send(events):
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    for e in events:
        body = json.dumps({"event": e, "sourcetype": "vmware:vsan:capacity", "index": e.get("index", "vmware")}).encode("utf-8")
        req = urllib.request.Request(HEC, data=body, method="POST")
        req.add_header("Authorization", "Splunk " + HECTOK)
        urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=30).read()
def rest_capacity(sess, base, cl):
    p = base + "/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/%s/capacity" % cl
    r = sess.open(p, timeout=30)
    return r.read()
def main():
    log = logging.getLogger("vsan")
    log.addHandler(logging.FileHandler("/var/log/vsan_cap_poll.log"))
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    ctx.load_verify_locations(CA)
    h = os.environ["VC"]
    u = os.environ["VU"]
    p = os.environ["VP"]
    si = connect.SmartConnect(host=h, user=u, pwd=p, sslContext=ctx)
    at = si.content.sessionManager.currentSession.key
    events = []
    for row in list_clusters_vsan_enabled(si):
        j = get_json_with_session("https://%s/rest" % h, at, row["rest_id"])
        events.append(flatten_capacity_json(h, j, row))
    send(events)
    connect.Disconnect(si)
    return 0
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

Flesh out `list_clusters_vsan_enabled`, REST session helper with `X-Auth` / `vmware-api-session-id` (see VMware docs for your vCenter), SOAP fallback to `vsanQuerySpaceUsage` on 7.0 U1 builds, and `/host` to compute largestHostCapacityB. On HTTP 503 back off; on 401 renew; on vSAN-Health 503 set `vsanHealthDegraded: true` in the JSON but still ship. Map `efficiencyBreakdown`. do not run this script on the vCenter VM; use a Linux UF or HF with outbound 443 to vCenter and 8088 to HEC.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Paste the `spl` field from this use case. Save, verify row counts, then Save As → Alert with a 5m or 10m schedule, `earliest=-25h@h`, and throttle on `vcenter, cluster` for non-critical. The opening `sourcetype` union is intentional: it documents the three HEC contracts you may deploy; the `where sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity"` line keeps the byte math on the primary feed (optional `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` is for sibling drill, not the severity core here).

**Search walkthrough** (plain language): the `coalesce()` chains exist because the vSAN-Health SOAP path historically emitted camelCase (`totalCapacityB`), the REST path emits snake\_case (`total_bytes`, `reserved_capacity` object sometimes flattened in your poller), and older backfills you wrote by hand may have used intermediate names—without `coalesce`, a rename in vCenter 8.0 U3 would silently zero out the alert. The `streamstats` window=288 with 5m polls gives ~24h of history so `used_growth_bytes_per_day` is the trailing 24h delta in bytes (not a spike in one bucket); when growth is 0, eta is null by design, not zero days. `stats` `latest()` per (vcenter, cluster) de-dupes the 5m cadence to one row for lookup join and freeze math. The `join` to `vmware:vsan:health` collapses the worst of the two test ids; red there forces critical in the `case` before generic thresholds, matching operations instinct that the native health test should win when it disagrees with a one-field typo in a JSON line. The `lookup` provides per-cluster SLO, not a global 80/90 for every object: archival or compliance clusters may sit at 88% usable by design; retail POS may cap at 60%.

Worked example (numbers only): total_bytes=100 TiB, reserved sum 30 TiB (policy + host-rebuild + operations as vCenter reports to your poller) ⇒ usable_capacity_bytes=70 TiB; used=63 TiB ⇒ used_pct=63% (looks “fine” on a legacy gauge) but usable_used_pct=90% (exactly the sort of value that blocks new VMs when hysteresis and hftt are tight). For largest host 12 TiB and free=15 TiB, headroom is +3 TiB; if free were 10 TiB, you are -2 TiB and the next full-host loss cannot be rebuilt in place.

`freeze`: `change_freeze_until` parses on ISO or date-only; `freeze_active` demotes any non-critical row to ok for pager routing so finance freeze windows are respected; a red vSAN test or <7 day eta still breaks the freeze. Wire ServiceNow on high and medium, Slack on low, and email on ok-only if you add `| where severity_bucket="ok"` in a second non-page report.

Canonical SPL (must match the `spl` field character-for-character in production):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.27 — vSAN cluster-level capacity headroom, slack/operations+host-rebuild reserve, usable_used_pct, host-rebuild headroom, ETA-to-full at 5m cadence. PRIMARY sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity from GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity (v7.0 U2+) or SOAP vsanQuerySpaceUsage; the base search union lists vmware:vsan:health (capacity-test slice) plus vmware:vsan:diskgroup (UC-19.2.17 drill contract); the numeric pipeline filters to vmware:vsan:capacity. NOT UC-19.2.17 per-DG state, NOT UC-19.2.23 witness, NOT per-DG used_pct for admission—usable_used_pct vs slack is the control plane.")`
(index=vmware OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup") earliest=-25h@h
| eval cluster=toString(coalesce(cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
| eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, vcenter_fqdn, ""))
| eval total_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_bytes, totalCapacityB, total_capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval free_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(free_bytes, freeCapacityB, free_capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval used_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_bytes, usedCapacityB, used_capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval reserved_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(reserved_bytes, reservedCapacityB, reserved_capacity_bytes, policy_reserve_capacity, policyReserveCapacity, "0")), 10)
| eval largest_host_capacity_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(largest_host_capacity_bytes, largestHostCapacityB, largestHostCapacity, "0")), 10)
| eval host_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_count, hostCount, "0")), 10)
| eval dedup_enabled=if((lower(toString(coalesce(dedup_enabled, dedupEnabled, "true")))="true") OR (toString(coalesce(dedup_enabled, dedupEnabled, "0"))="1"), 1, 0)
| eval dedup_ratio=tonumber(toString(coalesce(dedup_ratio, dedupRatio, efficiency_dedup_ratio, dedup_ratio_observed, "0")), 10)
| where (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity") AND (isnotnull(total_bytes)) AND (total_bytes>0)
| sort 0 + _time, vcenter, cluster
| streamstats window=288 current=t first(used_bytes) AS used_24h_ago avg(used_bytes) AS avg_used_bytes range(used_bytes) AS range_used_bytes BY vcenter, cluster
| eval used_growth_bytes_per_day=if((isnotnull(used_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(used_bytes)) AND (used_bytes>=used_24h_ago), used_bytes - used_24h_ago, 0)
| eval used_pct=round((used_bytes / total_bytes) * 100, 1)
| eval free_pct=round((free_bytes / total_bytes) * 100, 1)
| eval reserve_pct=if((isnotnull(reserved_bytes)) AND (total_bytes>0) AND (reserved_bytes>=0), round((reserved_bytes / total_bytes) * 100, 1), null())
| eval usable_capacity_bytes=if(isnotnull(reserved_bytes), total_bytes - reserved_bytes, total_bytes)
| eval usable_used_pct=if((isnotnull(usable_capacity_bytes)) AND (usable_capacity_bytes>0) AND (isnotnull(used_bytes)), round((used_bytes / usable_capacity_bytes) * 100, 1), null())
| eval headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes=if((isnotnull(free_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(largest_host_capacity_bytes)), free_bytes - largest_host_capacity_bytes, null())
| eval eta_full_days=if((used_growth_bytes_per_day>0) AND (isnotnull(free_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(reserved_bytes)) AND ((free_bytes - reserved_bytes) > 0), round((free_bytes - reserved_bytes) / used_growth_bytes_per_day, 1), null())
| stats max(_time) AS _time latest(used_bytes) AS used_bytes latest(free_bytes) AS free_bytes latest(used_24h_ago) AS used_24h_ago latest(used_growth_bytes_per_day) AS used_growth_bytes_per_day latest(used_pct) AS used_pct latest(free_pct) AS free_pct latest(reserve_pct) AS reserve_pct latest(usable_capacity_bytes) AS usable_capacity_bytes latest(usable_used_pct) AS usable_used_pct latest(headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes) AS headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes latest(eta_full_days) AS eta_full_days latest(dedup_ratio) AS dedup_ratio latest(dedup_enabled) AS dedup_enabled latest(largest_host_capacity_bytes) AS largest_host_capacity_bytes latest(reserved_bytes) AS reserved_bytes latest(avg_used_bytes) AS avg_used_bytes latest(range_used_bytes) AS range_used_bytes latest(host_count) AS host_count latest(total_bytes) AS total_bytes BY vcenter, cluster
| join type=left vcenter, cluster [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" earliest=-25h@h | eval h_test=lower(toString(coalesce(health_test_id, healthTestId, test_id, testId, ""))) | eval h_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(health_test_status, test_status, testStatus, overallStatus, resultStatus, "")))) | eval cluster=toString(coalesce(cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, cluster_id, clusterId, "")) | eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, "")) | where (like(h_test, "%clustercapacity%utilization%")) OR (like(h_test, "%objectquorum%objectquorum%")) OR (h_test="com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.clustercapacity.utilization") OR (h_test="com.vmware.vsan.health.test.cn.objectquorum.objectquorum") | eval w=if((like(h_status, "%red%")) OR (h_status="failed") OR (h_status="error"), 3, if((like(h_status, "%yellow%")) OR (h_status="warning"), 2, 1)) | stats max(w) AS w BY vcenter, cluster | eval cap_health_tier=case((w>2), "red", w=2, "yellow", 1=1, "green") | fields vcenter, cluster, cap_health_tier ]
| lookup vsan_cluster_capacity_owner cluster OUTPUT business_service, owner_email, expected_dedup_ratio, expected_host_count, change_freeze_until, capacity_target_pct, capacity_warn_pct, capacity_critical_pct, target_resync_window_hours
| eval t_crit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(capacity_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
| eval t_warn=tonumber(toString(coalesce(capacity_warn_pct, "80")), 10)
| eval t_tgt=tonumber(toString(coalesce(capacity_target_pct, "70")), 10)
| eval exp_dedup=tonumber(toString(expected_dedup_ratio), 10)
| eval exp_hc=tonumber(toString(expected_host_count), 10)
| eval cap_health_tier=if((isnotnull(cap_health_tier)) AND (len(toString(cap_health_tier))>0), cap_health_tier, "green")
| eval dedup_drift=if((isnotnull(dedup_ratio)) AND (dedup_ratio>0) AND (isnotnull(exp_dedup)) AND (exp_dedup>0) AND (dedup_ratio < (exp_dedup * 0.75)), 1, 0)
| eval low_host=if((isnotnull(exp_hc)) AND (exp_hc>0) AND (isnotnull(host_count)) AND (host_count < exp_hc), 1, 0)
| eval reserve_bloat=if((isnotnull(reserve_pct)) AND (reserve_pct > 30), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((cap_health_tier="red") OR (isnotnull(usable_used_pct) AND (usable_used_pct >= t_crit)) OR (isnotnull(headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes) AND (headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes < 0)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_full_days)) AND (eta_full_days < 7)), "critical", (cap_health_tier="yellow") OR (isnotnull(usable_used_pct) AND (usable_used_pct >= t_warn)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_full_days)) AND (eta_full_days < 30)) OR ((isnotnull(headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(largest_host_capacity_bytes)) AND (largest_host_capacity_bytes>0) AND (headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes < (largest_host_capacity_bytes * 0.25))), "high", (isnotnull(usable_used_pct) AND (usable_used_pct >= t_tgt)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_full_days)) AND (eta_full_days < 60)) OR (dedup_drift=1), "medium", (low_host=1) OR (reserve_bloat=1), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now() < coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (severity_bucket!="critical"), "ok", severity_bucket)
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical", 1, severity_bucket="high", 2, severity_bucket="medium", 3, severity_bucket="low", 4, 1=1, 5)
| convert ctime(_time) AS last_poll
| sort 0 sev_order, -usable_used_pct, cluster
| table _time, last_poll, vcenter, cluster, severity_bucket, sev_order, cap_health_tier, usable_used_pct, used_pct, free_pct, reserve_pct, headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes, largest_host_capacity_bytes, eta_full_days, used_growth_bytes_per_day, dedup_drift, low_host, reserve_bloat, business_service, owner_email, freeze_active, t_crit, t_warn, t_tgt
```

Why `usable_used_pct` is the “real” control-plane pressure: admission and policy reservation subtract from what applications may consume, so the denominator of interest is usable capacity after those slices, not total rack TiB.

**Step 3 — Validate**

Path A (operator UI): in vSphere Client, open the vSAN cluster → Monitor → vSAN → Capacity. Read used, free, and reserved (and overhead / vSAN versions-specific groupings). Compare the same cluster to the newest `vmware:vsan:capacity` event’s `_time`; differences <5 minutes are normal on 300s polls, >15 minutes means NTP or collector lag.

Path B (host CLI): on a selected ESXi member, SSH and run `esxcli storage core device list` (orientation) and `esxcli vsan storage list` (namespace visibility), then `vdq -i` and `vdq -q` as your runbook allows to validate physical capacity contributors match your HCL tier (cache vs capacity) without pasting the output into uncontrolled tickets.

Path C (RVC on vCenter or a jumphost): from `/usr/bin` RVC, attach to the vCenter and run the documented `vsan.cluster_info <path>` (release-specific) to dump the object-level summary VMware support will ask for in a sev-1. Screen-capture the RVC block into the change record, not the raw SPL row.

REST self-check: obtain a session token with `POST https://<vcenter>/api/session` and Basic auth (lab only), then `GET https://<vcenter>/api/vcenter/vsan/clusters` to list IDs, `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity` with header `vmware-api-session-id: <token>`; compare `total`/`used` fields to the poller. Revoke the session in lab when done. Do not embed passwords in the Splunk search string.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

Dashboard (single pane): (1) KPI row: usable_used_pct + eta_full_days + headroom_for_host_rebuild_bytes in one row per cluster; (2) line over 30d for used_bytes with an overlaid reserved_bytes band; (3) forecast off summary indexing of `used_growth_bytes_per_day` (keep it out of the alerting path if the math is not stable yet); (4) table sorted `sev_order`, `usable_used_pct` desc. ITSI (optional): map each `business_service` to a service with HVA and HVF in the KPI description text so the glass table can show the largest host headroom in the subtitle.

Alert recipes: PagerDuty on `severity_bucket=critical` with vCenter and cluster in the first line; ServiceNow P2 on high with `target_resync_window_hours` breach in the work note if headroom is <25% of largest host; Slack on medium; email digest on low (efficiency, host_count drift). Throttle: 1h on (vcenter, cluster, severity_bucket) except critical headroom <0 which re-notifies on 15m until acknowledged.

Runbook (short): (1) Add capacity in this order when possible: add drives in emptiest disk groups first if you are DG-bound, else add a node to grow N+1 headroom; (2) emergency evac: sDRS-aware vMotion of the largest low-RPO VMs to other clusters with vSAN policy parity; (3) quarter-close: use `change_freeze_until` in the CSV; if CAB must still acknowledge a red vSAN test, the SPL already lifts non-critical noise but leaves critical through.



Step 5 — Troubleshooting

 (a) `usable_used_pct` null ⇒ reserved_bytes or used_bytes not in the HEC line—open props; (b) eta null when you expect a number—used_growth is 0 (cold cluster) or <24h in index; (c) join to health always green but UI is yellow—test id filter too tight, widen the `like`; (d) largest host 0 on 8-node cluster—bad `/host` mapping; (e) alerts during LCM—freeze or maintenance tags; (f) RVC and SPL disagree—treat RVC as vendor truth for a TAC case, then fix the poller.

(g) `usable_used_pct` looks correct but `host_rebuild_pct` is null on a 4-node cluster: check `largest_host_used_bytes` in the HEC body — pyVmomi may emit the per-host capacity slice but a stale firmware can flatten it; restart the poller after a host add/remove. (h) `eta_to_full_h` jumps from null to 168h then back to null — natural when used_growth oscillates near zero on a quiet cluster; only treat sustained <168h as an actionable trend. (i) Witness-related capacity confusion: stretched-cluster witness has its own UC (UC-19.2.23) and does not appear in this cluster-capacity row even though vSphere reports a witness datastore — keep them separate. (j) Sudden `dedup_ratio_drift` after policy change: re-baseline by clearing the previous 24h `streamstats` window and capturing a fresh `dedup_ratio_baseline` after the policy stabilizes. (k) Disk-group degradation appears in UC-19.2.17 but not here: correct — per-disk-group health is its own UC; this UC is the cluster-capacity sibling. (l) During LCM rolling-host upgrade, `usable_used_pct` may temporarily climb because one host's capacity is offline; if `change_freeze_until` is set, severity demotes to `ok` automatically. (m) `vmware:vsan:health` test id filter is too tight: vendor renames test ids across vSphere 7→8; widen the `match()` regex to handle both `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.controlleronhcl` and the 8.x `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.controllerruntime`.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full the shared storage really is after the system keeps space for big repairs and moves—like a clear spot in a packed moving van for a hand truck. If you only count boxes, you think there is room when the next move or repair would still be blocked. We help the right people see that margin in time, before new work, moves, or snapshots are refused.
hardware: Applies to vSAN ReadyNodes and partner HCI nodes on the VMware compatibility list, including Dell PowerEdge R650 / R750 / R760 ReadyNodes, HPE ProLiant DL360 / DL380 Gen10/Gen11 ReadyNodes, Lenovo ThinkAgile VX series, Cisco UCS C-Series ReadyNodes, Fujitsu PRIMERGY RX and CX vSAN lines, Dell VxRail (the Dell OEM appliance; vSAN is the storage), Hitachi UCP HC where vSAN is the storage plane, and other OEM vSAN appliances. Cache tier is enterprise NVMe or SAS SSD (high write endurance), capacity tier may be read-optimized SATA SSD or 7.2K in hybrid models. ESA on vSphere 8+ expects homogeneous high-performance NVMe for the architecture you purchased—do not expect OSA/ESA to share identical efficiency math in Splunk. Always compare hardware generations against the vSAN HCL before expanding.
